header.stance: Front-end-led Treasury rally eased near-term pressure while long-end premia stayed firm
header.title: Daily FI Market Intelligence

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 9 Treasury close delivered a bull-steepening reversal of the previous two sessions’ inflation-and-supply selloff. Front-end and belly yields fell more than the long bond as crude retraced, incoming labor data separated stable layoffs from slower re-employment, and John Williams described a Fed reaction function that still allows patience if underlying inflation resumes a benign path. The move softened the immediate case for an accelerated tightening cycle without removing the premium attached to long maturity, fiscal supply and energy uncertainty.

The most important change from July 8 was the market’s treatment of the renewed US-Iran conflict. Oil had previously pushed the expected policy response forward and raised real yields; Thursday’s decline in crude allowed part of that impulse to unwind. The rates rally was still selective. Ten-year real yields did not fall, and the long end recovered less than policy-sensitive maturities. Refined-fuel margins remained elevated and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was severely impaired, leaving a credible route from geopolitics to consumer inflation even after the daily commodity reversal.

Treasury supply produced a constructive counterforce. The 30-year reopening cleared below its pre-auction yield despite awarding investors the highest rate on the tenor since 2007. That result extended the week’s pattern of concession attracting end demand, but it did not erase the structural competition between sovereign borrowing and AI-related corporate financing. The long bond’s limited rally and the persistence of high long-end real yields show that successful placement is different from a durable reduction in the required return on duration.

Credit and cross assets treated the day as managed inflation risk, not a funding break. Investment-grade and high-yield listed returns edged higher, equities rallied sharply in technology, volatility declined and the dollar softened. At the same time, distressed issuers continued to require creditor coordination and balance-sheet repair, while Asian sovereign and corporate borrowers used offshore markets to reinforce liquidity or fund capital spending. The close therefore replaced the prior day’s broad restrictive-rate impulse with a narrower distinction: near-term Fed pressure eased, yet the long-run cost of funding remained elevated and highly sensitive to supply, energy transmission and issuer quality.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasuries rallied across the curve, led by policy-sensitive maturities. The two-year yield fell five basis points to 4.16%, the five-year declined four to 4.27%, the 10-year eased two to 4.54%, and the 30-year slipped one to 5.05%. The ordering matters because it reverses Wednesday’s belly-led cheapening without producing a comparable long-duration recovery. Lower crude and a conditional Fed message reduced the immediate policy premium, while fiscal supply and persistent inflation risk kept the far end above the levels reached late in June.

The daily gains repaired only part of the week’s damage. Two-year yields were still two basis points higher over one week, compared with increases of four at five years, five at 10 years and seven at 30 years. The one-month changes were more dispersed: the five- and 10-year yields were each two basis points lower, the two-year was one basis point higher, and the 30-year remained two basis points higher. Duration pressure has therefore accumulated with maturity even though Thursday’s catalyst favored a rally.

Curve slopes captured that separation. The 2s10s spread widened three basis points to 38, and 2s30s widened four to 89. The 2s5s10s butterfly declined one basis point to minus 16 as the five-year outperformed its wings, while the 2s10s30s butterfly rose two to minus 13 because the 10-year retained more relative support than the long bond. The close was a front-end-led bull steepener layered onto a weekly pattern of progressively larger long-end losses.

Inflation-linked Treasuries show that falling inflation compensation, not lower real yields, accounted for the benchmark nominal rally. The 10-year real yield held at 2.31%, while the 10-year breakeven declined two basis points to 2.23%. Five-year real yields eased one basis point to 1.99%, but seven-, 20- and 30-year real yields were unchanged at 2.14%, 2.66% and 2.86%. The market marked down some near-term energy pass-through while preserving the high real return demanded beyond the front end.

Jobless claims supplied a mixed labor signal consistent with that modest policy relief. Initial claims fell 2,000 to 215,000 in the week ended July 4, and their four-week average dropped to 218,750. Continuing claims rose 8,000 to 1.814 million for the week ended June 27, taking the four-week average to 1.808 million, while the insured unemployment rate stayed at 1.2%. Layoffs remained contained, but the rise in continuing claims suggested that workers were taking longer to find new jobs. That combination does not indicate a sharp demand break; it does reduce the force of an exclusively resilient-labor interpretation.

Williams kept the reaction function explicitly two-sided. The New York Fed president said sustained AI-driven demand relative to supply could create inflation that monetary policy should address. He also identified a monthly core PCE pace of 0.2% during the second half as consistent with continued disinflation and a return toward the target. His framing left support for hikes conditional on persistence, complementing the claims detail and crude decline without declaring the inflation problem resolved.

The 30-year reopening showed that yield concession could still mobilize demand. The bonds were awarded at 5.058%, below the 5.061% yield in pre-auction trading shortly before the bidding deadline. The award was the highest since 2007, yet the stronger-than-anticipated clearing level helped Treasury yields retreat from session highs. It extended the constructive results of the three- and 10-year sales earlier in the week and limited the long bond’s loss of relative value during the session.

Absorption did not eliminate the long-end burden. The new bonds had traded as high as 5.095% earlier in the day, and the closing 30-year real yield remained near the elevated level reached during the selloff. Government borrowing is competing with a corporate market financing very large AI infrastructure programs, raising the amount of duration that investor balance sheets must carry. A successful auction can stabilize the tape at a given concession even as the equilibrium clearing rate remains high.

Bills displayed a sharp distinction between overnight cash conditions and the outer policy horizon. The four-week coupon-equivalent yield rose six basis points to 3.69%, while the six-week fell two to 3.68% and the eight-week held at 3.71%. Thirteen-, 17- and 26-week yields declined four, three and four basis points to 3.78%, 3.86% and 3.92%; the 52-week yield fell three to 4.02%. The larger decline from three months onward aligned with the easing in the two-year sector, whereas the four-week cheapening was maturity-specific.

Thursday’s bill auctions were absorbed at large announced sizes, with a stronger bidder mix in the eight-week tenor. The 28-day sale accepted $100.0003887 billion at a 3.630% high rate and 2.64 bid-to-cover; indirect bidders received 56.61%, direct bidders 4.62% and dealers 38.77%. The 56-day sale accepted $95.0002726 billion at 3.635% with 2.95 coverage; indirects took 74.57%, directs 4.46% and dealers 20.98%. Both issue and settle July 14, keeping the auction outcome distinct from the coming cash transfer.

Late-session banking liquidity was supportive at the margin but arrived too late to explain the earlier rally. The Federal Reserve reported at 4:30 p.m. ET that average reserve balances rose $132.0 billion to $3.099 trillion in the week ended July 8 as the average Treasury General Account fell $106.2 billion to $774.1 billion. Reverse repos increased $4.0 billion to $348.8 billion. The release informed only the final half-hour of the fixed-income session, where it reinforced a liquid funding backdrop without changing the macro origin of the move.

The rates close consequently separated three forces that had moved together on July 8. Softer crude and lower breakevens reversed part of the inflation shock; mixed claims and Williams’s conditional threshold reduced near-term hike urgency; and strong long-bond demand capped the supply premium. Flat benchmark and long-end real yields prevented the rally from becoming a full duration reset. The curve still requires compensation for fiscal borrowing, AI-linked financing and the possibility that refined-fuel pressure keeps inflation persistent after crude’s daily retreat.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Listed credit returns were modestly positive alongside the Treasury rally and stronger equities. LQD gained 0.04%, HYG rose 0.11%, and JNK added 0.10%. The limited high-grade advance reflected the small underlying duration move, while the similar gains in the two high-yield funds showed that the day did not produce broad lower-quality risk aversion. Issuer cases remained much more dispersed than the aggregate gauges.

AI infrastructure continued to expand the scale and complexity of credit supply. A $35 billion financing package arranged by Apollo and Blackstone for Broadcom and Anthropic uses a special-purpose vehicle to buy custom chips and lease them to Anthropic. Broadcom backstops payments on the largest senior portions, and the delayed-draw design divides funding into about 16 releases as chips become available. The structure broadens access beyond conventional unsecured technology bonds, but it also creates a large new pool of equipment-linked credit whose liquidity and performance will matter as drawn pieces enter institutional portfolios.

Leveraged-finance pipelines showed that underwritten capacity remained available for transactions with identifiable assets and uses of proceeds. Barclays was preparing roughly $875 million of debt for a takeover of UK aerospace supplier Senior, including two senior secured term facilities and a revolving line. Well Health was seeking C$150 million of five-year high-yield bonds through a private placement to repay a convertible bond due this year and fund general purposes. Both cases tied borrowing to acquisition or liability management, giving investors a clearer cash-use framework than open-ended expansion financing.

Distressed issuers faced a different market. Vestel Elektronik hired Houlihan Lokey to consider alternatives for its $500 million dollar bond due in May 2029 while renegotiating local bank loans. The bond fell about seven cents to 52 cents on the dollar after Fitch had cut the issuer two notches to CCC- and called its capital structure unsustainable. Bank loans account for roughly three quarters of debt, so an eventual solution must coordinate domestic lenders and Eurobond holders as revenue pressure and higher labor, material and logistics costs weaken cash generation.

Aston Martin’s creditor organization reinforced the same recovery-value focus. An ad hoc group holding more than half of its senior secured notes signed a cooperation agreement as the $1.05 billion 2029 dollar bond traded near 72 cents, down about four cents during Wednesday’s move. First-quarter revenue growth did not prevent a £65.5 million pretax loss or net debt from reaching £1.46 billion. Security remains valuable, but claim priority alone cannot offset a recurring need for cash injections when operating performance and leverage deteriorate together.

Credit access was therefore open at several points on the quality spectrum, with structure deciding the terms. Listed funds participated in the benign risk tone, large private lenders engineered asset-backed AI capacity, and acquisition banks prepared secured distribution. Distressed borrowers required adviser-led repair and coordinated creditors. The common constraint was the cost of capital: high base yields and abundant competing supply reward strong sponsorship, collateral and specific refinancing objectives, while weak issuers face a rapid shift from ordinary funding to recovery negotiations.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

European duration recovered as crude calmed, though policy pricing stayed restrictive. Two-year Bund yields fell two basis points to 2.68% after a 12-basis-point rise on Wednesday, and gilt yields declined by as much as three basis points across the curve. German trade data added a growth offset: May exports increased 0.9% to €137.9 billion, imports fell 2.5% to €118.8 billion, and the seasonally adjusted surplus widened to €19.1 billion from €14.7 billion. Stronger external demand limited the case for a purely defensive duration rally.

ECB expectations remained firm despite the oil retreat. Swaps assigned a 90% probability to a September increase, and the chance of another move by year-end was close to evenly divided. Core-inflation momentum, elevated refined-fuel costs and a wider German surplus left policymakers with both price and activity reasons for caution. The Bund move therefore resembled a partial reversal of Wednesday’s shock, not a decisive reopening of an easing cycle.

Japan supplied the more persistent global term-premium channel. The BOJ’s July regional assessment left all nine headline judgments unchanged, with every region describing recovery or pickup despite weakness in some areas. Employment and income were improving throughout the country, and business investment was increasing or remained high. Kanto-Koshinetsu added a Middle East-related weakness qualifier. The overall picture supported continued normalization while acknowledging that imported energy and geopolitical disruption can weaken activity as well as lift prices.

The five-year JGB auction attracted decent demand at elevated yields. Bid-to-cover rose to 3.43, slightly above the 12-month average of 3.35, and the gap between the average and lowest accepted prices narrowed to 0.03 from 0.06 in June. Futures weakened after the result, the five-year yield remained close to 2%, and the 10-year traded around 2.9%, its highest since 1996. Investors absorbed the security, yet fiscal spending plans, a weak yen and concern that BOJ tightening is lagging inflation continued to burden longer maturities.

The UK faced an analogous buyer-base question at the far end. Investors doubted that the Debt Management Office would complete the full £22.4 billion slate of long-dated issuance planned for the fiscal year after no bond with at least 15 years to maturity had been sold during the first three months. The remit allows changes in maturity mix and distribution method. Any reallocation can reduce immediate pressure in the long sector, though it also signals that elevated yields and volatile demand are constraining the sovereign’s preferred funding schedule.

Europe’s debate over common borrowing added a strategic supply dimension. Germany and France questioned Spain’s proposal to centralize more national issuance at the EU level, even as Christine Lagarde argued for a European asset capable of competing with Treasuries. The proposal envisaged a debt stock near €5 trillion within five years and potential annual interest savings of as much as €25 billion for governments. A deeper common market could improve liquidity and global reserve appeal, but it would also create a large unified duration pool competing for the same investors as national bonds and US Treasuries.

Global duration thus shared the US pattern of successful placement without full term-premium relief. German and UK yields recovered as oil eased, Japanese auction demand improved, and official activity data remained resilient. Fiscal programs and policy normalization still require a high clearing rate across markets. For Treasuries, the transmission runs through comparative yields, currency-hedged returns and the amount of sovereign and infrastructure duration global portfolios must absorb at once.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market dollar debt benefited from a slightly softer common backdrop. EMB rose 0.18% to $95.96 as the US Dollar Index slipped 0.11% to 100.94 and Treasury yields declined. That combination eased the daily discount-rate and currency burden, but country funding outcomes continued to depend on reserve needs, commodity exposure and the credibility of debt-management plans.

South Korea used favorable offshore conditions to strengthen its foreign-currency position. The sovereign sold €1.7 billion of foreign-exchange stabilization notes, comprising a €700 million three-year tranche at 10 basis points over mid-swaps and a €1 billion seven-year tranche at 28 basis points. Both priced about four basis points through guidance and at the tightest spreads the sovereign has achieved. Issuing earlier than its usual second-half schedule replenished reserves that had been used to support the won and established a benchmark for other Korean borrowers.

Indonesia illustrated the opportunity and the fragility created by energy sensitivity. Foreign inflows into rupiah sovereign bonds reached a 13-month high in June after higher yields helped stabilize the currency. Two- and 10-year yields stood at 7.29% and 7.31% on Wednesday, offering substantial carry, yet the favorable case depends on lower oil reducing imported inflation and the need for additional Bank Indonesia tightening. Renewed Hormuz disruption would reverse that mechanism most directly for large energy importers.

Asian central banks remained differentiated. Bank of Korea nominee Shin Hyun-song told lawmakers that a rate increase would be needed at an appropriate time because inflation, improving growth and financial-stability risks were pointing in the same direction. Bank Negara Malaysia held its overnight policy rate at 2.75%, with contained inflation and a firmer currency allowing patience. The contrast shows that better regional risk tone does not create a uniform local-duration path; domestic demand and exchange-rate pass-through determine how much relief lower US yields can transmit.

China’s local-government debt conversion reduced one refinancing hazard while preserving the underlying fiscal burden. Issuance to swap hidden liabilities into official local-government bonds had reached about 94% of a 6 trillion-yuan allowance by the end of June, after regions raised more than 1.62 trillion yuan in the first half. Replacing costlier LGFV borrowing with government bonds improves transparency and near-term servicing economics. It also shifts more debt onto public balance sheets while spending constraints and weak domestic demand limit the growth dividend.

The EM close was consequently supportive at the index level and selective beneath it. Korea demonstrated tight hard-currency access, Indonesia drew local inflows at high yields, and Malaysia retained policy flexibility. Korea’s prospective tightening and China’s debt migration underscored how inflation and fiscal risks remain country-specific. A softer dollar can ease the common constraint for one session; reserve use, energy imports and the maturity structure of public liabilities decide whether that relief endures.

## Cross asset

Equities treated the session as a contained geopolitical shock and a renewed AI-growth trade. The S&P 500 gained 0.81%, the Nasdaq 100 rose 1.62%, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index advanced 3.06%. The VIX fell 6.27% to 15.84. That combination reinforced the front-end Treasury rally by reducing the need to price an immediate disorderly escalation, yet strong technology demand also preserved the investment and financing impulse highlighted by Williams.

Energy delivered the clearest reversal. WTI fell 1.96% to $72.08 and Brent declined 2.20% to $76.30 after the prior session’s surge. Both remained higher over one week, and the gasoline crack spread near $57 a barrel showed that cheaper crude had not normalized the cost of refined products. Severe disruption to Hormuz traffic and attacks on Russian refining capacity kept fuel availability tighter than the headline oil close alone suggested.

Gold rose 1.47% to $4,130.60 as nominal yields declined and geopolitical uncertainty persisted. The dollar’s 0.11% fall reduced the currency headwind for bullion and emerging-market assets. Gold’s gain alongside equities and lower volatility was therefore compatible with lower rates and residual event hedging, not a broad liquidation into havens.

The cross-asset constellation supported a daily easing of the inflation-policy shock while leaving its medium-term transmission unresolved. Falling crude, a softer dollar, stronger stocks and lower volatility aligned with lower Treasury yields and firmer listed credit. Elevated refined-fuel margins, high real yields and enormous AI capital needs complicated the relief. Markets accepted the premise of managed conflict for the close, but the price of duration still reflected the possibility that energy supply or investment demand becomes persistent enough to keep policy restrictive.

## Forward Market Significance

June CPI and real earnings on July 14 at 8:30 a.m. ET, followed by June PPI on July 15 at 8:30 and retail sales plus initial claims on July 16 at 8:30, form the next US reaction-function test. Cooling core and services inflation, limited producer-price pass-through and softer consumption or continuing re-employment friction would extend Thursday’s front-end rally and validate Williams’s benign disinflation branch, with two- to five-year yields and breakevens most sensitive. Firm underlying prices, resilient control-group spending and another low initial-claims reading would challenge the view that the oil reversal materially reduced policy urgency, restoring upward pressure on real yields even if crude remains below its wartime peak.

The $171 billion combined 13- and 26-week Treasury bill sales on July 13 at 11:30 a.m. ET and the $95 billion 42-day sale on July 14 at 11:30 measure whether money-market cash can absorb another large wave ahead of July 16 settlement. Japan’s roughly ¥700 billion 20-year JGB auction on July 14 adds the global-duration test. Strong coverage, broad nondealer participation and stable secondary trading would extend Thursday’s auction message that concession is drawing cash across maturities and jurisdictions, easing pressure on funding markets and the long end. Dealer-heavy outcomes or renewed post-sale cheapening would challenge that interpretation and reopen the buyer-base concern embedded in the elevated 30-year Treasury and JGB real-rate hurdle.

The EIA petroleum report on July 15 at 10:30 a.m. ET and natural-gas storage report on July 16 at 10:30 isolate the energy channel that separated crude from refined products at Thursday’s close. Rebuilding gasoline and distillate stocks, easier crack spreads, steady refinery operation and another gas injection that preserves the five-year inventory cushion would confirm that the daily oil decline can flow into lower inflation compensation. Further product draws, refinery constraints or erosion of the gas cushion would challenge that relief even if headline crude is stable, pushing breakevens and policy-sensitive yields higher and testing the resilience of credit and equities to a more persistent household-fuel shock. The combination of weekly crude performance and inventory detail will reveal whether the bond market is seeing a temporary headline retracement or a genuine repair in the fuel-supply mechanism that reaches consumers.

section_summaries.market_read: Near-term tightening pressure eased, but the close did not deliver broad long-duration relief.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether fuel disruption revives inflation compensation while long-end supply stays heavy.
section_summaries.summary: Thursday’s bull steepener reversed part of the inflation-and-supply selloff as crude retreated, labor signals softened at the margin and Fed patience remained possible. Yet unchanged real yields, impaired Hormuz traffic and heavy sovereign and corporate issuance preserved a sizable premium for long maturity.

section_summaries.market_read: The curve priced less near-term policy urgency without lowering the structural long-end hurdle.
section_summaries.next_check: The next check is whether inflation data lower real yields as well as breakevens.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Policy-sensitive Treasuries outperformed as lower breakevens, mixed claims and Williams’s conditional framework eased immediate hike urgency. Strong auction demand capped the selloff, but unchanged benchmark and long-end real yields showed that fiscal borrowing, AI financing and fuel risk still command elevated duration compensation.

section_summaries.market_read: Risk tone improved at the index level, but funding access remained sharply quality dependent.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether new secured deals distribute cleanly as distressed credits pursue repairs.
section_summaries.summary: Listed credit participated modestly in the benign close, while financing remained available for sponsored, secured and purpose-specific deals. Large AI structures expanded supply, and distressed borrowers still required advisers, creditor coordination and balance-sheet repair, keeping issuer quality decisive under high base rates.

section_summaries.market_read: Global duration echoed Treasuries: better absorption stabilized markets without clearing term premia.
section_summaries.next_check: Track whether upcoming sovereign auctions draw broad demand without post-sale cheapening.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: European yields partly reversed the oil shock and Japanese auction demand improved, but restrictive policy expectations and fiscal issuance kept global term premia elevated. Sovereign funding plans and possible maturity reallocations showed that successful placement can coexist with persistent buyer-base pressure across major duration markets.

section_summaries.market_read: Common conditions eased, yet reserve needs and energy exposure still governed EM resilience.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether lower oil sustains Indonesian inflows without forcing renewed policy tightening.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: A softer dollar and lower Treasury yields supported emerging-market debt, but country outcomes remained tied to reserves, energy imports and fiscal credibility. Korea accessed euros tightly, Indonesia drew local inflows at high yields, while policy divergence and China’s debt migration limited a uniform duration benefit.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets confirmed daily relief from the inflation shock, not resolution of its transmission.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch refined-fuel availability for signs that cheaper crude reaches household inflation.
section_summaries.summary: Equities, technology and listed credit advanced as crude and volatility fell, supporting the view that geopolitical inflation risk was contained for the session. Gold still rose, refined-fuel margins stayed elevated and AI capital demand remained strong, preserving both event hedging and long-run financing pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: The rally now depends on disinflation, durable auction demand and genuine fuel-supply repair.
section_summaries.next_check: Compare the next data, auction follow-through and inventory detail with Thursday’s drivers.
section_summaries.summary: Three tests will challenge the close: US inflation and activity data, bill and JGB supply, and petroleum inventories. Softer prices, broad auction participation and rebuilding fuel stocks would extend the rally, while firm inflation, dealer-heavy sales or product draws would restore pressure through rates and risk assets.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.31% (0 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.23% (-2 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 38 bp (+3 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 89 bp (+4 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Falling crude and conditional Fed patience drove a selective Treasury bull steepener
overall_summary.session_narrative: The NY close reversed part of the prior two-session selloff, with front-end and belly yields falling more than the long bond. Crude’s retreat, lower breakevens, mixed claims and a conditional Fed message reduced the immediate policy premium, while firm equities, softer volatility and modest credit gains signaled managed inflation risk rather than funding stress. Unchanged benchmark real yields, elevated refined-fuel margins and heavy sovereign and AI-linked supply limited the duration reset.
overall_summary.summary: Policy-sensitive yields led the rally as lower crude, mixed labor signals and a two-sided Fed message reduced immediate tightening urgency. Stable real yields and a smaller long-bond gain kept fiscal supply, energy pass-through and duration costs firmly in view.

watch_events.market_implication: That mix would extend the two- to five-year rally and lower breakevens.
watch_events.market_trigger: Cooling prices with softer consumption or slower re-employment would confirm disinflation.
watch_events.title: US inflation and activity test
watch_events.watch_line: Track core and services inflation, producer pass-through, spending and labor turnover.

watch_events.market_implication: That outcome would ease funding and long-end pressure; dealer-heavy sales would reopen it.
watch_events.market_trigger: Strong nondealer demand with stable post-sale pricing would confirm cash absorption.
watch_events.title: Money-market and global duration auctions
watch_events.watch_line: Assess coverage, bidder breadth and secondary trading across bill and JGB supply.

watch_events.market_implication: Breakevens and policy-sensitive yields would ease; renewed draws would pressure risk assets.
watch_events.market_trigger: Product rebuilding and steady refining would show that crude relief is reaching fuel supply.
watch_events.title: Petroleum inventory and refining test
watch_events.watch_line: Track product stocks, crack spreads, refinery operation and the gas inventory cushion.
